context:
Create a multi-chain configuration registry at backend/src/config/chains.ts. This is the foundation for Polygon and Arbitrum support (CEO issue #42 - Sprint 10 Multi-chain architecture).

The file should export:
1. A SUPPORTED_CHAINS constant: a Map<string, ChainConfig> with entries for:
   - 'base': { id: 8453, name: 'Base', rpcUrl: process.env.BASE_RPC_URL || 'https://mainnet.base.org', usdcAddress: '0x833589fCD6eDb6E08f4c7C32D4f71b54bdA02913', explorerUrl: 'https://basescan.org', isTestnet: false }
   - 'polygon': { id: 137, name: 'Polygon', rpcUrl: process.env.POLYGON_RPC_URL || 'https://polygon-rpc.com', usdcAddress: '0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359', explorerUrl: 'https://polygonscan.com', isTestnet: false }
   - 'arbitrum': { id: 42161, name: 'Arbitrum One', rpcUrl: process.env.ARBITRUM_RPC_URL || 'https://arb1.arbitrum.io/rpc', usdcAddress: '0xaf88d065e77c8cC2239327C5EDb3A432268e5831', explorerUrl: 'https://arbiscan.io', isTestnet: false }
2. A getChain(chainId: string): ChainConfig function that throws if chain not found
3. A isChainSupported(chainId: string): boolean helper
4. A DEFAULT_CHAIN = 'base' constant
5. A ChainConfig interface with: id (number), name (string), rpcUrl (string), usdcAddress (string), explorerUrl (string), isTestnet (boolean)

Also update backend/src/types/index.ts (or create it if it doesn't exist) to export the ChainConfig type.
acceptance_criteria: getChain('base'), getChain('polygon'), getChain('arbitrum') all return valid ChainConfig objects. getChain('unknown') throws.
Migration file is valid SQL. POST /v1/invoices accepts chain field. Invalid chain returns 400.

context:
Finalize the Invoica TypeScript SDK for npm publication as @invoica/sdk (CEO issue #45).

The SDK source is in sdk/ directory. Create or update the following files:

1. sdk/package.json — production-ready package manifest:
   { "name": "@invoica/sdk", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "Official TypeScript SDK for Invoica — Financial OS for AI Agents", "main": "dist/index.js", "types": "dist/index.d.ts", "exports": { ".": { "import": "./dist/index.js", "types": "./dist/index.d.ts" } }, "files": ["dist", "README.md"], "scripts": { "build": "tsc", "prepublishOnly": "npm run build" }, "keywords": ["invoica", "x402", "ai-agents", "payments", "usdc", "blockchain"], "license": "MIT", "peerDependencies": {} }

2. sdk/tsconfig.json — TypeScript config for building:
   { "compilerOptions": { "target": "ES2020", "module": "CommonJS", "lib": ["ES2020"], "declaration": true, "declarationMap": true, "sourceMap": true, "outDir": "./dist", "rootDir": "./src", "strict": true, "esModuleInterop": true, "skipLibCheck": true }, "include": ["src/**/*"], "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", "**/*.test.ts"] }

3. sdk/src/index.ts — main SDK entry: re-export everything from client.ts, types.ts

4. sdk/src/types.ts — comprehensive TypeScript types:
   - Invoice interface (id, number, amount, currency, status, customerId, dueDate, chain, createdAt, updatedAt, metadata)
   - CreateInvoiceRequest interface
   - Settlement interface (id, invoiceId, txHash, chain, amount, currency, status, confirmedAt)
   - InvoiceStatus enum: 'draft' | 'sent' | 'paid' | 'overdue' | 'cancelled'
   - ListInvoicesParams interface (status?, customerId?, page?, limit?, chain?)
   - InvoicaConfig interface (apiKey: string, baseUrl?: string)

5. sdk/src/client.ts — InvoicaClient class:
   - constructor(config: InvoicaConfig) — stores apiKey and baseUrl (default: 'https://api.invoica.ai')
   - async createInvoice(data: CreateInvoiceRequest): Promise<Invoice>
   - async getInvoice(id: string): Promise<Invoice>
   - async getInvoiceByNumber(number: string): Promise<Invoice>
   - async listInvoices(params?: ListInvoicesParams): Promise<{ invoices: Invoice[], total: number, page: number }>
   - async updateInvoice(id: string, data: Partial<CreateInvoiceRequest>): Promise<Invoice>
   - async trackSettlement(invoiceId: string): Promise<Settlement>
   - private async request<T>(method: string, path: string, body?: unknown): Promise<T> — uses native fetch (Node 18+), adds Authorization: Bearer header, handles errors

6. sdk/README.md — quickstart guide:
   - Installation: npm install @invoica/sdk
   - Quick start with 3 examples: create invoice, list invoices, track settlement
   - TypeScript example showing full flow
   - Link to https://invoica.ai/docs
acceptance_criteria: sdk/src/client.ts exports InvoicaClient class with all 7 methods. sdk/package.json has name @invoica/sdk version 1.0.0.

context:
Create an API key rotation service at backend/src/services/api-key-rotation.ts (GitHub issue #36 - AUTH-002: Create API key rotation service).

The service should:
1. Export an ApiKeyRotationService class with:
   - async rotateKey(oldKeyId: string, userId: string): Promise<{ newKey: string, newKeyId: string, expiresOldKeyAt: Date }>
     - Generates a new secure API key (format: 'ik_live_' + 32 random hex chars)
     - Inserts new key into api_keys table (Supabase)
     - Sets old key's expires_at to NOW() + 24 hours (grace period)
     - Returns the new key and its ID
   - async revokeKey(keyId: string, userId: string): Promise<void>
     - Sets is_active = false on the key immediately
     - Only allows if userId owns the key (security check)
   - async listKeys(userId: string): Promise<ApiKey[]>
     - Returns all active keys for user (never returns the raw key value, only prefix + last 4 chars)
2. Export an ApiKey interface: { id: string, userId: string, prefix: string, lastFour: string, createdAt: Date, expiresAt: Date | null, isActive: boolean }
3. Use the Supabase client from backend/src/lib/supabase.ts (or wherever it's defined)

Also add the rotation endpoint to backend/src/routes/api-keys.ts:
   POST /v1/api-keys/:id/rotate — calls rotateKey, returns { apiKey: newKey, keyId: newKeyId, expiresOldKeyAt }
   DELETE /v1/api-keys/:id — calls revokeKey
   GET /v1/api-keys — calls listKeys, returns { keys: ApiKey[] }
acceptance_criteria: ApiKeyRotationService class exported with rotateKey, revokeKey, listKeys methods. Route file exports router with 3 endpoints.
